ogTitle: Woronora Heights vs Worrigee: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Woronora Heights and Worrigee.
heading: Woronora Heights vs Worrigee — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Woronora Heights, New South Wales and Worrigee, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Worrigee has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Woronora Heights (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Woronora Heights or Worrigee safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Woronora Heights has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Worrigee scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Woronora Heights scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Worrigee scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Woronora Heights or Worrigee?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Worrigee has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
